name: Overflow User Flow Diagramming Tool API
what_it_does: User flow diagramming and presentation tool with REST API — enabling UX designers to create detailed user flow diagrams by importing screens from Figma, Sketch, or Adobe XD, connecting them with flow arrows and conditions, and presenting user journeys to stakeholders through clickable flow demonstrations with built-in presentation mode.
use_cases: Importing design screens from Figma via Overflow API to create user flow diagrams
Managing Overflow user flow projects and boards via API for team collaboration
Exporting Overflow user flow diagrams as images or PDF via API for documentation
Integrating Overflow flows with project management tools for design documentation
Querying user flow project structure for design audit and documentation coverage
not_for: High-fidelity interactive prototyping (use Figma or Proto.io for interactive prototypes)
Design system management (use Zeroheight or Frontify for design system documentation)
Real-time collaborative design (use Figma for live design collaboration)
best_when: A UX designer needs to clearly communicate user journeys and app flows to non-technical stakeholders — creating professional flow presentations that import real design screens from Figma without the complexity of full prototyping tools.
avoid_when: You need interactive prototypes, real-time collaboration, design-to-code handoff, or are already using Figma's FigJam for flow diagramming.

auth.notes: API key from Overflow account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks. Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD import via design tool integrations. Desktop application (Mac + Windows) for primary flow editing. Web sharing for stakeholder presentation. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.notes: Israeli company (Tel Aviv). Founded 2017. Bootstrapped. Niche product focused specifically on user flow documentation as a distinct deliverable from prototypes. Known for beautiful presentation mode for user flows. Primarily appeals to UX designers who want professional flow diagrams without raw diagramming tools. Competition from Figma FigJam reducing demand. Desktop application as primary interface. Small but profitable niche.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited REST API for project management. API key bearer auth. Minimal public API documentation. No webhooks. Primary value is desktop application and web presentation, not API integration. Israeli company with GDPR awareness. Niche product with limited API investment.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Flow reads idempotent. Project creation not idempotent — check by name before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Very limited public API — primary product value is desktop/web UI, not API integration
Desktop application required for full flow creation — web-only has limitations
Figma FigJam market pressure — evaluate if Overflow's specialized flow tools justify cost
Israeli company — verify EU data residency requirements
No webhooks — must poll for project change events

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. API key auth. Israeli company with GDPR awareness. Flow diagrams may contain unreleased product designs — restrict sharing access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Shared flows accessible via URL — configure access controls.
